content:
Case 9:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 81
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
- Vital signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination of the right shoulder and upper arm"}}

response.request: physical examination of the right shoulder and upper arm

content:
History Physical:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "routine blood work including CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel, serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation, and serum free light chain assay"}}

response.request: routine blood work including CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel, serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation, and serum free light chain assay

content:
'protein_studies' available in round 2.
The SPEP/IFE and serum free light chains are in the protein studies item, which becomes available in a later round. You can instead request the CBC (cbc_coagulation) and serum chemistries (chemistry_immunology) now if you want routine blood work immediately.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "CBC with coagulation and serum chemistry panel (electrolytes, renal function, liver enzymes, calcium, albumin, total protein)"}}

response.request: CBC with coagulation and serum chemistry panel (electrolytes, renal function, liver enzymes, calcium, albumin, total protein)

content:
Cbc Coagulation:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "radiographic imaging of the right shoulder (plain X\u2011ray of the glenohumeral joint)"}}

response.request: radiographic imaging of the right shoulder (plain X‑ray of the glenohumeral joint)

content:
'radiographic imaging of the right shoulder (plain X‑ray of the glenohumeral joint)' not available. Try 'ct_skeleton' instead.
This case doesn't list a dedicated plain radiograph item. You can request skeletal imaging findings (ct_skeleton), which covers imaging of bones and related reports available in this round.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "ct_skeleton"}}

Ct Skeleton:
2025-07-03: CT skeleton showed multiple osteolytic lesions of the axial and appendicular skeleton, most prominent in vertebrae, ribs, and pelvis, without signs of new destruction, consistent with inactive post-treatment myeloma lesions. Soft-tissue swelling at the right shoulder and proximal arm incompletely characterized due to lack of contrast.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "ultrasound examination of the right shoulder and proximal arm with Doppler"}}

response.request: ultrasound examination of the right shoulder and proximal arm with Doppler

content:
'ultrasound examination of the right shoulder and proximal arm with Doppler' not available. Try 'ct_skeleton' instead.
This case does not include an ultrasound of the shoulder/proximal arm. A related imaging item available in this round is CT skeleton; consider requesting that for skeletal/osseous evaluation.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "protein_studies"}}

'protein_studies' available in round 2.
The case includes serum/urine protein studies (SPEP/UPEP, immunofixation, free light chains) but those results are scheduled for a later round. Consider requesting labs or imaging available in this round (e.g., CBC, chemistry/immunology, CT skeleton, or abdominal ultrasound).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum chemistry panel (electrolytes, renal function, liver enzymes, calcium, albumin, total protein)"}}

response.request: serum chemistry panel (electrolytes, renal function, liver enzymes, calcium, albumin, total protein)

content:
Chemistry Immunology:
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.

solve.working_diagnosis: Active multiple myeloma with extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder causing localized swelling and pain

treatment_plan.action: Contrast‑enhanced MRI of the right shoulder and proximal arm to delineate soft‑tissue mass and extent of bone involvement
Bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy for cellularity and plasma‑cell percentage
Start intravenous isotonic hydration and administer zoledronic acid 4 mg weekly to control hypercalcemia and prevent renal damage
Administer oral dexamethasone 20 mg daily for 4 weeks to reduce tumor burden and alleviate pain
Initiate a bortezomib‑based regimen (e.g., bortezomib 1.3 mg/m², lenalidomide 25 mg, dexamethasone) once disease activity is confirmed and renal function is optimised

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetic analysis"}}

response.request: bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetic analysis

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow smears were normocellular with approximately 5-10 % plasma cells. The plasma cells appeared large with eccentric nuclei, coarse “clock-face” chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and abundant basophilic cytoplasm. Occasional binucleated forms were noted. The background marrow showed mildly reduced granulopoiesis and dysmorphic erythropoiesis, with scattered small lymphocytes and occasional mast cells. No excess of blasts was identified. Flow cytometry revealed 0.9 % plasma cells with strong expression of CD38⁺, CD138⁺, and CD27⁺ and dim CD56 expression, showing cytoplasmic kappa light-chain restriction. CD19 and CD45 were negative. No aberrant myeloid or B-cell populations were detected, 1.2% CD34low blasts.
Histology (core biopsy) demonstrated age-adjusted normocellular marrow (overall cellularity 40 %) with nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10 % of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138⁺ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction, negative for lambda. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Relapsed multiple myeloma with extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder", "differentials": ["Metastatic bone cancer (e.g., prostate or breast carcinoma)", "Lymphoma with bone involvement (e.g., diffuse large B\u2011cell lymphoma)", "Osteomyelitis of the proximal humerus", "Soft\u2011tissue sarcoma such as liposarcoma"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate aggressive intravenous hydration 3\u202fL/day and administer zoledronic acid 4\u202fmg IV once weekly to control hypercalcemia and protect renal function", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Obtain contrast\u2011enhanced MRI of the right shoulder and proximal arm to delineate the soft\u2011tissue mass and extent of bone involvement", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Start a bortezomib (1.3\u202fmg/m\u00b2 SC every 2\u202fweeks) plus dexamethasone (20\u202fmg weekly) regimen, add lenalidomide 25\u202fmg days 1\u201121 of a 28\u2011day cycle with dose adjustment for renal function", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Plan localized radiation therapy to the right shoulder mass (30\u202fGy in 10 fractions) once MRI and staging are complete", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed multiple myeloma with extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder
differentials: Metastatic bone cancer (e.g., prostate or breast carcinoma)
Lymphoma with bone involvement (e.g., diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma)

treatment_plan.action: Initiate aggressive intravenous hydration 3 L/day and administer zoledronic acid 4 mg IV once weekly to control hypercalcemia and protect renal function
Obtain contrast‑enhanced MRI of the right shoulder and proximal arm to delineate the soft‑tissue mass and extent of bone involvement
Start a bortezomib (1.3 mg/m² SC every 2 weeks) plus dexamethasone (20 mg weekly) regimen, add lenalidomide 25 mg days 1‑21 of a 28‑day cycle with dose adjustment for renal function
Plan localized radiation therapy to the right shoulder mass (30 Gy in 10 fractions) once MRI and staging are complete

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Specialist hematology conference (definitive diagnosis and treatment planning)

You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and transplant scores to establish the final diagnosis, risk stratification, and a realistic treatment and follow-up strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetic/molecular workup (FISH for del(17p), t(4;14), t(14;16), 1q21 amplification) and PET\u2011CT staging to assess extra\u2011osseous disease extent"}}

response.request: cytogenetic/molecular workup (FISH for del(17p), t(4;14), t(14;16), 1q21 amplification) and PET‑CT staging to assess extra‑osseous disease extent

content:
Cytogenetics Fish:
FISH analysis revealed trisomy 3, t(4;14)(p16;q32) involving IGH::FGFR3/MMSET, deletion 13q14, deletion of CDKN2A, gain of 1q21, and deletion of 17p.
Conventional cytogenetics demonstrated a complex karyotype in the CD138-negative fraction.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Relapsed, high\u2011risk IgA\u03ba multiple myeloma with an extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder", "differentials": ["Metastatic bone disease (e.g., prostate or breast carcinoma)", "Diffuse large B\u2011cell lymphoma involving bone", "Osteomyelitis of the proximal humerus", "Soft\u2011tissue sarcoma"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Contrast\u2011enhanced MRI of the right shoulder and proximal arm to delineate the soft\u2011tissue mass and any cortical bone disruption", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Contrast\u2011enhanced PET\u2011CT for global staging and to document extra\u2011osseous disease burden", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Local radiotherapy to the right shoulder mass (30\u202fGy in 10 fractions) to control local pain and reduce mass effect", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Start a daratumumab (16\u202fmg/kg IV weekly, then every 2 weeks) + pomalidomide (1.5\u202fmg daily for 21 days of each 28\u2011day cycle) + dexamethasone (20\u202fmg weekly) regimen with dose reduction for eGFR\u00a0<\u00a030\u202fmL/min/1.73\u202fm\u00b2", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate intravenous hydration (3\u202fL/day) and zoledronic acid 4\u202fmg IV once weekly to control hypercalcemia and protect renal function", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Provide prophylactic antibiotics (e.g., levofloxacin 500\u202fmg PO daily) during the first cycle and G\u2011CSF support if neutropenia develops", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Schedule follow\u2011up PET\u2011CT after 3 cycles to assess radiographic response and adjust therapy accordingly", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Offer palliative care consult to manage pain, fatigue, and discuss goals of care given age and comorbidities", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed, high‑risk IgAκ multiple myeloma with an extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder
differentials: Metastatic bone disease (e.g., prostate or breast carcinoma)
Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma involving bone

treatment_plan.action: Contrast‑enhanced MRI of the right shoulder and proximal arm to delineate the soft‑tissue mass and any cortical bone disruption
Contrast‑enhanced PET‑CT for global staging and to document extra‑osseous disease burden
Local radiotherapy to the right shoulder mass (30 Gy in 10 fractions) to control local pain and reduce mass effect

Start a daratumumab (16 mg/kg IV weekly, then every 2 weeks) + pomalidomide (1.5 mg daily for 21 days of each 28‑day cycle) + dexamethasone (20 mg weekly) regimen with dose reduction for eGFR < 30 mL/min/1.73 m²
Initiate intravenous hydration (3 L/day) and zoledronic acid 4 mg IV once weekly to control hypercalcemia and protect renal function
Provide prophylactic antibiotics (e.g., levofloxacin 500 mg PO daily) during the first cycle and G‑CSF support if neutropenia develops
Schedule follow‑up PET‑CT after 3 cycles to assess radiographic response and adjust therapy accordingly
Offer palliative care consult to manage pain, fatigue, and discuss goals of care given age and comorbidities
